posts.text: More fraudulent: Bam Adebayo's 83 or Michael Bam Jordan's Oscar Award

“When Houston offered me the job, I said, you’ve got to find a way to get around this nepotism thing because if I can’t hire my son and my daughter, then I don’t think it’ll work.” - Houston head coach, Kelvin Sampson, on the importance of his children to his staff.

Dan, I get your guy Bruce Pearl is off to a very rough start. Had no business attacking Miami to benefit Auburn. No business cussing at refs from the stands. He’s made a fool of himself. He’s not likable. Doesn’t seem to have grown much since secretly recording Deon Thomas. Having you attack Clark and Seth might be a new low for Pearl.

Agreed Clark is a fine person but knows absolutely NOTHING ABOUT HOOPS

Agreed Clark is a fine person but knows absolutely NOTHING ABOUT HOOPS

CBS execs made the switch immediately after a year of “that’s right Steve”
Don’t believe me?
Producer Steve Sheer knows 
Cmon big man at least know a little

I was joking (perhaps too roughly) with Dan late last week. 

I can't for the life of me figure out why he's trashing Clark Kellogg. Clark epitomizes class and professionalism.

You 2 brainiacs going at it again …

Clark Kellog is one of the best human beings on the planet. All class! Knows the game and follows it closely. 
I've argued with Seth Davis over his fake wokeness, but he's solid as an information journalist. 

They're not trying to be First Take or McAfee.


Dan Dakich (@dandakich)

Bruce Pearl is the only interesting person on the selection show

Seth Davis?
DEI hire
He/She/They/Them
On air for 20 years has yet to say one  interesting informative thing.. ass kis

I hear ya. But there's no real controversy with OU. Small snubbed. Not a big deal. 

This may be the new normal. Only the mid majors can really get snubbed. OU will just buy another batch of players for next season. It's not like a large group of 4-year seniors just had their dream killed.

Josh Howerton, pastor of Lakepointe Church, urged cohabiting couples during a sermon to honor God by getting married.

After the message, 52 couples came forward, met with pastors for counseling, and were married together in a joint ceremony.

The church later celebrated the couples, with more than 1,000 people attending a service to applaud their decision to repent and commit to marriage.
